# Termicolor - Extended Documentation

> Complete guide to generating terminal color schemes from images

Website: https://termicolor.io

## Table of Contents

1. Overview
2. Features in Detail
3. ANSI Color System
4. Export Format Specifications
5. URL Sharing Protocol
6. Accessibility Features
7. Use Cases
8. FAQ

---

## 1. Overview

Termicolor is a free, privacy-focused web application for generating terminal color schemes from images. It runs entirely in the browser with no server-side processing.

**Key Benefits:**
- Create cohesive terminal themes that match your wallpaper or brand
- Ensure accessibility with built-in WCAG contrast checking
- Export to any major terminal emulator
- Share themes via URL

---

## 2. Features in Detail

### Color Extraction
- Uses median-cut color quantization algorithm
- Extracts 8-16 dominant colors from any image
- Supports JPEG, PNG, WebP, and GIF formats
- No file size limits (processed locally)

### Color Mapping
The extracted colors are intelligently mapped to ANSI color slots:
- Background and foreground colors
- 8 normal colors (black, red, green, yellow, blue, magenta, cyan, white)
- 8 bright variants of each color
- Cursor and selection colors

### Live Preview
- Realistic terminal simulation
- Shows actual code with syntax highlighting
- Dark and light mode toggle
- Displays how colors work together

### Profile Management
- Save multiple color profiles locally
- Import existing iTerm2 .itermcolors files
- Quick switching between saved themes

---

## 3. ANSI Color System

Standard 16-color ANSI palette used by Termicolor:

| Index | Name         | Typical Use                    |
|-------|--------------|--------------------------------|
| 0     | Black        | Background, dark elements      |
| 1     | Red          | Errors, deletions              |
| 2     | Green        | Success, additions, strings    |
| 3     | Yellow       | Warnings, modifications        |
| 4     | Blue         | Information, links, keywords   |
| 5     | Magenta      | Special, constants             |
| 6     | Cyan         | Strings, special text          |
| 7     | White        | Default text                   |
| 8-15  | Bright variants of 0-7                      |

---

## 4. Export Format Specifications

### iTerm2 (.itermcolors)
- XML plist format
- Contains RGB values as floating-point (0.0-1.0)
- Includes all 16 ANSI colors plus UI colors

### Alacritty (YAML)
- YAML configuration snippet
- Hex color values
- Copy into alacritty.yml under `colors` section

### Kitty (conf)
- Plain text configuration
- Format: `color0 #hexvalue`
- Append to kitty.conf

### Windows Terminal (JSON)
- JSON color scheme object
- Add to `schemes` array in settings.json
- Reference by name in profile

### Hyper (JavaScript)
- JavaScript configuration object
- Add to .hyper.js config

### Terminal.app
- macOS terminal profile
- Import via Terminal preferences

---

## 5. URL Sharing Protocol

Termicolor supports sharing color schemes via URL parameters.

### Format
```
https://termicolor.io/?c=<base64-encoded-colors>
```

### Encoding Details
- Colors are encoded as a compact binary format
- Base64 encoded for URL safety
- Contains all 16 ANSI colors + UI colors (background, foreground, cursor, selection)
- Total: 20 colors × 3 bytes (RGB) = 60 bytes before encoding

### Example Workflow
1. Create or modify a color scheme
2. Click "Copy URL" or share button
3. URL contains encoded colors
4. Recipients see the exact same colors when they visit

---

## 6. Accessibility Features

### WCAG Contrast Analysis
- Checks contrast ratio between foreground and background
- Checks each ANSI color against background
- Reports WCAG AA and AAA compliance levels

### Minimum Contrast Ratios
- WCAG AA: 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text
- WCAG AAA: 7:1 for normal text, 4.5:1 for large text

### Auto-Fix
- Automatic adjustment of colors that fail contrast checks
- Preserves hue while adjusting lightness
- Optional manual override

---

## 7. Use Cases

### For Developers
- Match terminal theme to IDE or wallpaper
- Create branded terminal themes for teams
- Ensure accessible color schemes for visibility

### For Content Creators
- Generate consistent themes for tutorials
- Create themed terminal screenshots
- Share configurations with followers

### For Designers
- Quickly prototype terminal color schemes
- Test color palette accessibility
- Export to multiple platforms at once

---

## 8. FAQ

**Q: Is my image uploaded to a server?**
A: No. All image processing happens in your browser. Nothing is sent to any server.

**Q: Can I use this offline?**
A: Yes. After the initial page load, the app works entirely offline.

**Q: What image formats are supported?**
A: JPEG, PNG, WebP, and GIF.

**Q: How do I import an existing iTerm2 theme?**
A: Click the import button and select your .itermcolors file.

**Q: Can I manually adjust individual colors?**
A: Yes. Click any color swatch in the palette to open a color picker.

**Q: Is this free?**
A: Yes, completely free with no ads or tracking.

**Q: How do I report a bug or suggest a feature?**
A: Visit the website and use the feedback option.

---

## Technical Specifications

- Built with React and TypeScript
- Uses ColorThief for color extraction
- Runs on Cloudflare's edge network
- No cookies or tracking
- Mobile-responsive design
- Source images never leave your device

---

- Website: https://termicolor.io
- Documentation: https://termicolor.io/llms.txt
- AI Plugin: https://termicolor.io/.well-known/ai-plugin.json
